message:
feat(python): numpy.polynomial.legendre wrapper parity

Mirrors the chebyshev (7 + 6 wrappers) and hermite/laguerre (13 each,
via prior sweeps) Python-side surface to cover every small-API
numpy.polynomial.legendre helper: legadd / legsub / legmul, legval
(with the tensor kwarg), legroots, legfromroots, legpow (with the
maxpower guard), legdiv (tuple return), legline, legmulx, legtrim
(with tol), legder (m / scl / axis), legint (m / k / lbnd / scl /
axis). All 13 are direct passthroughs.

A single live-NumPy parity test exercises the arithmetic triad across
four (same-length, both-mismatched, scalar) cases, legval at scalar /
vector / 2-D coefficient matrix with tensor=False, legroots on
quadratic / cubic / length-1 constant, legfromroots across 0..3 roots,
legpow at 0..3 plus the maxpower-violation ValueError parity path,
legdiv tuple-part-wise compare, legline across three (off, scl) pairs,
legmulx both against numpy and against our legmul(c, [0, 1]) identity,
legtrim with tol=0 and a positive tolerance that trims near-zero
trailing coefficients, legder at m=0..2, legint with every kwarg set,
and a legder(legint(c)) round-trip parity check.

Closes the orthogonal-polynomial-basis wrapper sweep for chebyshev /
hermite / laguerre / legendre; remaining gaps in each basis are the
2-D/3-D grid evaluators, vandermonde builders, quadrature helpers, and
fitting routines. Still orthogonal to panes 2/4's numpy.ma /
numpy.testing domains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
